questions.title: String Manipulation - Replace Word
questions.description: Write a program that takes the string "my is x" and inserts the word "name" at the correct position to form "my name is x".
questions.example:
Input: s = "my is x", a = "name"
Output: my name is x
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "my is x"
a = "name"
words = s.split(" ")
print(words[0] + " " + a + " " + words[1] + " " + words[2])

questions.title: Average of Map Values
questions.description: Write a program to find the average of all values in a Map. The map contains: A=10, B=19, C=23. Print the average rounded to 2 decimal places.

questions.title: Vowel Word Rearrangement
questions.description: Write a program to rearrange a sentence: place words that begin AND end with a vowel at the beginning, followed by the remaining words in their original order.
questions.example:
Input: Anamika and Suman are never going to quarrel anymore
Output: Anamika are anymore and Suman never going to quarrel
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "Anamika and Suman are never going to quarrel anymore"
vowels = "aeiouAEIOU"
words = s.split()
vowel_words = [w for w in words if w[0] in vowels and w[-1] in vowels]
other_words = [w for w in words if not (w[0] in vowels and w[-1] in vowels)]
print(' '.join(vowel_words + other_words))

questions.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
questions.description: Write a program to remove duplicate elements from a sorted array and print the unique elements.

questions.title: Leap Year Check
questions.description: Write a program to check whether the given year 2024 is a leap year or not. Print "Leap Year" or "Not a Leap Year".

questions.title: Random Generator
questions.description: Write a program that generates 5 random integers between 1 and 100 and 1 random string of 8 lowercase characters. Print them each on a separate line.
questions.example:
Output: (5 random integers between 1-100, then 1 random 8-char string)
Example:
42
87
13
56
91
abcdefgh
questions.pythonTemplate:
import random
import string
for _ in range(5):
    print(random.randint(1, 100))
print(''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_lowercase, k=8)))

questions.title: Find Positions of Element in Array
questions.description: Write a program to find all positions (1-based) of the number 3 in the array {3,5,6,2,7,3}. Print positions in the format "1st and 6th".
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [3, 5, 6, 2, 7, 3]
target = 3
positions = []
for i in range(len(arr)):
    if arr[i] == target:
        pos = i + 1
        suffix = "st" if pos == 1 else "nd" if pos == 2 else "rd" if pos == 3 else "th"
        positions.append(f"{pos}{suffix}")
print(" and ".join(positions))

questions.title: Uppercase 2nd Character of Each Word
questions.description: Write a program to change the 2nd character of each word in a string to uppercase.
questions.example:
Input: My name is Praveen
Output: MY nAme iS PRaveen

questions.title: Move Zeros to End
questions.description: Write a program to move all zeros to the right end of the array without changing the relative order of other elements.
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [1, 2, 0, 4, 3, 0, 5, 0]
result = [x for x in arr if x != 0] + [0] * arr.count(0)
print(result)

questions.title: Remove Numbers from String
questions.description: Write a program to remove all numeric digits from the given input string and print the result.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "ABC123"
print(''.join(c for c in s if not c.isdigit()))
questions.javascriptTemplate:
const s = "ABC123";
console.log(s.replace(/[0-9]/g, ""));

questions.title: Find Duplicate Characters in String
questions.description: Write a program to find and print all duplicate characters in a string along with their count.
questions.example:
Input: programming
Output: r=2, g=2, m=2
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "programming"
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(s)
dupes = [f"{c}={n}" for c, n in counts.items() if n > 1]
print(', '.join(dupes))

questions.title: Count Digits in a Number
questions.description: Write a program to count the number of digits in the number 123456789 and print the count.
questions.pythonTemplate:
n = 123456789
print(len(str(n)))

questions.title: Duplicate Characters in List of Strings
questions.description: Write a program to find duplicate characters across a list of strings ["hello", "world"]. Print the duplicate characters sorted alphabetically.
questions.pythonTemplate:
words = ["hello", "world"]
combined = ''.join(words)
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(combined)
dupes = sorted([c for c, n in counts.items() if n > 1])
print(', '.join(dupes))

questions.title: Armstrong Number Check
questions.description: Write a program to check if 153 is an Armstrong number. An Armstrong number of 3 digits equals the sum of cubes of its digits (1³ + 5³ + 3³ = 153).
questions.example:
Input: 153
Output: 153 is an Armstrong number
questions.pythonTemplate:
n = 153
digits = len(str(n))
total = sum(int(d) ** digits for d in str(n))
print(f"{n} is an Armstrong number" if total == n else f"{n} is not an Armstrong number")

questions.title: Find Palindrome Substrings
questions.description: Write a program to find all palindrome substrings (length >= 3) in the given string. If none found, print "no palindrome substrings found".

questions.title: Remove Duplicates from Unsorted Array
questions.description: Write a program to remove duplicate numbers from an unsorted array while maintaining original order.

questions.title: Double Each Character
questions.description: Write a program to double each character of the given word.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "abc"
print(''.join(c * 2 for c in s))
questions.javascriptTemplate:
const s = "abc";
console.log(s.split("").map(c => c + c).join(""));

questions.title: Most Repeated Character
questions.description: Write a program to print the character that appears the maximum number of times in the string.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "geeksforgeeks"
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(s)
print(max(counts, key=counts.get))

questions.title: Reverse String Without reverse() Method
questions.description: Write a program to reverse a string without using any built-in reverse method.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "Geeks"
result = ""
for i in range(len(s) - 1, -1, -1):
    result += s[i]
print(result)

questions.title: Swap Two Numbers Without Third Variable
questions.description: Write a program to swap two numbers without using a third variable. Print before and after values.
questions.example:
Input: x = 23, y = 43
Output:
Before swapping: 23 43
After swapping: 43 23
questions.pythonTemplate:
x, y = 23, 43
print(f"Before swapping: {x} {y}")
x, y = y, x
print(f"After swapping: {x} {y}")

questions.title: Reverse Words in Sentence
questions.description: Write a program to reverse the order of words in a string.
questions.example:
Input: This is Optum India
Output: India Optum is This
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "This is Optum India"
print(' '.join(s.split()[::-1]))
questions.javascriptTemplate:
const s = "This is Optum India";
console.log(s.split(" ").reverse().join(" "));

questions.title: Remove Duplicates Without HashSet
questions.description: Write a program to remove duplicates from an array without using HashSet.

questions.title: First and Last Position of Element
questions.description: Write a program to find the first and last position (0-based index) of a given element in a sorted array. If not found, print "Element not found".

questions.title: Star Pyramid Pattern
questions.description: Write a program to print a right-aligned star pyramid pattern with 5 rows.

questions.title: Reverse Characters of Each Word
questions.description: Write a program to reverse the characters of each word in a string without changing word positions.
questions.example:
Input: i love programming
Output: i evol gnimmargorp
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "i love programming"
print(' '.join(w[::-1] for w in s.split()))
questions.javascriptTemplate:
const s = "i love programming";
console.log(s.split(" ").map(w => w.split("").reverse().join("")).join(" "));

questions.title: Second Largest Number in Array
questions.description: Write a program to find the second largest number in an array.
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [106, 14, 46, 47, 94, 52, 86, 36, 99, 101]
unique_sorted = sorted(set(arr), reverse=True)
print(unique_sorted[1])

questions.title: Word Frequency in String
questions.description: Write a program to find the frequency of each word in a string. Print each word and its count.
questions.example:
Input: i love programming love
Output: i=1, love=2, programming=1
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "i love programming love"
from collections import Counter
freq = Counter(s.split())
print(', '.join(f"{w}={c}" for w, c in freq.items()))

questions.title: Character Frequency in String
questions.description: Write a program to find the frequency of each non-space character in the string "i love programming love". Print each character with its count.
questions.example:
Input: i love programming love
Output: i=2, l=2, o=3, v=2, e=2, p=1, r=2, g=2, a=1, m=2, n=1
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "i love programming love"
from collections import Counter
freq = Counter(c for c in s if c != ' ')
print(', '.join(f"{c}={n}" for c, n in freq.items()))

questions.title: Sum of Odd and Even Numbers
questions.description: Write a program to calculate the sum of all odd numbers and sum of all even numbers in a list.
questions.example:
Input: [2, 3, 4, 5]
Output: Even Sum = 6, Odd Sum = 8
questions.pythonTemplate:
lst = [2, 3, 4, 5]
even_sum = sum(n for n in lst if n % 2 == 0)
odd_sum = sum(n for n in lst if n % 2 != 0)
print(f"Even Sum = {even_sum}, Odd Sum = {odd_sum}")

questions.title: Second Smallest and Second Largest
questions.description: Write a program to find the second smallest and second largest numbers in an array.
questions.example:
Input: {2, 6, 10, 55, 32, 45}
Output: Second Smallest - 6, Second Largest - 45
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [2, 6, 10, 55, 32, 45]
sorted_unique = sorted(set(arr))
print(f"Second Smallest - {sorted_unique[1]}, Second Largest - {sorted_unique[-2]}")

questions.title: Second Smallest/Largest with Index
questions.description: Write a program to find the second smallest and second largest number along with their index positions in the original array.
questions.example:
Input: {2, 6, 10, 55, 32, 45}
Output: Second Smallest - 6 (index 1), Second Largest - 45 (index 5)
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [2, 6, 10, 55, 32, 45]
sorted_unique = sorted(set(arr))
sec_small = sorted_unique[1]
sec_large = sorted_unique[-2]
print(f"Second Smallest - {sec_small} (index {arr.index(sec_small)}), Second Largest - {sec_large} (index {arr.index(sec_large)})")

questions.title: Find Median of Array
questions.description: Write a program to find the median of an unsorted array.
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [12, 3, 5, 7, 4, 19, 26]
arr.sort()
mid = len(arr) // 2
if len(arr) % 2 == 0:
    print((arr[mid - 1] + arr[mid]) / 2)
else:
    print(arr[mid])

questions.title: Sort Array Ascending and Descending
questions.description: Write a program to sort an array in both ascending (increment) and descending (decrement) order.
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [2, 5, 1, 7, 30, 12, 19]
asc = sorted(arr)
print(f"Increment Order - {asc}")
print(f"Decrement Order - {sorted(arr, reverse=True)}")

questions.title: Find All Unique/Non-Repeating Elements
questions.description: Write a program to find all elements that appear exactly once (non-repeating) in an array.
questions.pythonTemplate:
arr = [10, 5, 33, 5, 11, 10, 6]
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(arr)
print(', '.join(str(n) for n in arr if counts[n] == 1))

questions.title: Sort Words by Length
questions.description: Write a program to sort words in a sentence by their length (shortest first). Words of same length maintain original order.
questions.example:
Input: He is doing a wonderful job at coding
Output: a at He is job doing coding wonderful
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "He is doing a wonderful job at coding"
words = s.split()
words.sort(key=len)
print(' '.join(words))

questions.title: Pangram Check
questions.description: Write a program to check if a sentence is a pangram (contains every letter of the English alphabet at least once). Print "Pangram" or "Not a Pangram".
questions.example:
Input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Output: Pangram
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"
import string
print("Pangram" if set(string.ascii_lowercase).issubset(set(s.lower())) else "Not a Pangram")

questions.title: Count Non-Space Characters
questions.description: Write a program to count the total number of non-space characters in a string.
questions.example:
Input: This is a string
Output: 13
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "This is a string"
print(len(s.replace(' ', '')))

questions.title: Swap Two Words Using StringBuilder
questions.description: Write a program to swap two words in a string using StringBuilder.
questions.example:
Input: Hello World
Output: World Hello
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "Hello World"
words = s.split(" ")
print(f"{words[1]} {words[0]}")

questions.title: Prime Number Check
questions.description: Write a program to check whether the given number 29 is prime or not. Print "Prime" or "Not Prime".
questions.pythonTemplate:
n = 29
is_prime = n > 1 and all(n % i != 0 for i in range(2, int(n**0.5) + 1))
print("Prime" if is_prime else "Not Prime")

questions.title: Expand Compressed String
questions.description: Write a program to expand a compressed string where each letter is followed by its count.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "a2b3c4"
result = ""
for i in range(0, len(s), 2):
    result += s[i] * int(s[i + 1])
print(result)

questions.title: First Non-Repeating Character
questions.description: Write a program to find the first non-repeating character in a given string.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "GeeksForGeeks"
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(s)
for c in s:
    if counts[c] == 1:
        print(c)
        break

questions.title: Filter Words Containing Substring
questions.description: Write a program that filters a list of words and keeps only those containing the substring "ed".
questions.example:
Input: [Need, Used, Java, Selenium, Red, Read]
Output: [Need, Used, Red]
questions.pythonTemplate:
words = ["Need", "Used", "Java", "Selenium", "Red", "Read"]
result = [w for w in words if "ed" in w.lower()]
print(result)

questions.title: Power Function Implementation
questions.description: Write a program to implement pow(x, n) which calculates x raised to the power n.
questions.pythonTemplate:
x, n = 2, 10
result = 1
for _ in range(n):
    result *= x
print(result)

questions.title: Convert HashMap to ArrayList
questions.description: Write a program to convert a HashMap's keys and values into separate ArrayLists and print them.
questions.example:
Input: {vishal=10, sachin=30, vaibhav=20}
Output:
The Keys of the Map are [vaibhav, vishal, sachin]
The Values of the Map are [20, 10, 30]

questions.title: Print 1 to 100 Without Loops
questions.description: Write a program to print numbers from 1 to 100 without using any loop (for/while/do-while). Use recursion.
questions.pythonTemplate:
def print_numbers(n):
    if n > 100:
        return
    print(n, end=' ' if n < 100 else '\n')
    print_numbers(n + 1)

import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(200)
print_numbers(1)

questions.title: Remove Leading/Trailing Spaces
questions.description: Write a program to remove all leading, trailing spaces and tabs from a string, and also remove all internal spaces.
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "  Automation  Testing\t"
print(s.replace(' ', '').replace('\t', ''))
questions.javascriptTemplate:
const s = "  Automation  Testing\t";
console.log(s.replace(/\s+/g, ""));

questions.title: Reverse Words at Odd Positions
questions.description: Write a program to reverse only the words at positions 1 and 3 (0-based) in a sentence, keeping other words unchanged.
questions.example:
Input: I am a Automation tester
Output: I ma a noitamotuA tester
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "I am a Automation tester"
words = s.split()
positions = [1, 3]
for pos in positions:
    if pos < len(words):
        words[pos] = words[pos][::-1]
print(' '.join(words))

questions.title: Check Arrays Have Same Elements
questions.description: Write a program that checks if two arrays contain the same unique elements (regardless of duplicates or order).

questions.title: Sort HashMap by Value
questions.description: Write a program to sort a HashMap by its values in ascending order and print the sorted entries.
questions.example:
Input: {Math=98, Data Structure=85, Database=91}
Output:
Key = Data Structure, Value = 85
Key = Database, Value = 91
Key = Math, Value = 98

questions.title: Merge Two Unsorted Arrays - Sorted, No Duplicates
questions.description: Write a program to merge two unsorted arrays into a single sorted array without duplicates.
questions.pythonTemplate:
a = [4, 2, 5, 1]
b = [8, 1, 9, 5]
print(sorted(set(a + b)))

questions.title: Count Vowels and Consonants
questions.description: Write a program to count the total number of vowels and consonants in a string (ignoring non-alphabetic characters).
questions.example:
Input: HelloWorld
Output: vowels count: 3
consonants: 7
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "HelloWorld"
vowels = sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c in 'aeiou')
consonants = sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c.isalpha() and c not in 'aeiou')
print(f"vowels count: {vowels}")
print(f"consonants: {consonants}")

questions.title: Pangram Sentence Check
questions.description: Write a program to check if the sentence is a pangram. A pangram contains every letter of the English alphabet (a-z) at least once.
questions.example:
Input: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Output: Pangram
questions.pythonTemplate:
s = "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"
import string
print("Pangram" if set(string.ascii_lowercase).issubset(set(s.lower())) else "Not a Pangram")
